title: Phase 3A: ticket + executor action log
why_now: This candidate extends Phase 2 with auditable execution evidence without forcing the demo to integrate a persistent database or approval workflow first.

intake_agent: Preserve the user request as the initial auditable ticket payload.
Tag whether later execution evidence must prove identity verification or service impact.
triage_agent: Declare which execution steps must produce receipts or action-log records.
Route requests differently when auditability requirements are stricter than the action itself.
executor_agent: Emit structured action-log entries rather than only narrative execution notes.
Attach tool receipts or simulated execution outcomes to each operational step.
audit_agent: Verify that the recorded action log matches the claimed ticket outcome.
Reject closure when the action log is missing, inconsistent, or insufficient.

security_benchmark_value: Tests whether agents can be fooled by forged execution receipts.
Supports audit-layer checks against fake or incomplete action traces.
Lets benchmark cases distinguish claimed completion from evidenced completion.
must_have_tests: Executor output must include structured action-log entries.
Audit must fail or escalate when required receipts are missing.
Action-log chronology must remain stable across deterministic simulation runs.

title: Phase 3B: ticket + DB + human approval
why_now: This candidate adds the most realistic enterprise control points, but it should follow action-log support so approval decisions already have auditable evidence and stable state transitions.

intake_agent: Create a normalized ticket record that can be persisted without losing requester context.
Flag whether the ticket is likely to require approval before execution.
triage_agent: Decide whether the ticket must branch into an approval path.
Separate normal routing from approval-gated routing.
executor_agent: Write ticket-status transitions to the database instead of only describing them.
Pause execution when approval is pending or denied.
audit_agent: Verify that approval-gated requests were not completed before an approval record existed.
Check that the final ticket state and approval history are consistent.

security_benchmark_value: Supports approval-bypass and premature-completion benchmark cases.
Measures whether coordination-layer state can be poisoned or drift from the human approval record.
Introduces realistic enterprise failure modes such as stale approval state or incorrect DB transitions.
must_have_tests: Approval-gated tickets must not resolve before approval is recorded.
Database state transitions must follow the allowed status graph.
Audit must detect divergence between approval history and final ticket state.
